sglang.title: Fix per_token_group_quant_8bit when hidden_dim // group_size is not divided by 4.
sglang.author: strgrb
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

As mentioned in #8290 , per_token_group_quant_8bit has the issue when hidden_dim // group_size is not divided by 4. It is fixed by this pr and set runtime type to __nv_fp8_e4m3 to improve performance.
Now the performance is quite close to the one in #8290 , but still a little bit slower.

The model output could be json object with plain text. e.g:

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Mistral crashes on tool with no description
vllm.author: HugoMichard
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #21165.
Adds a default empty description string to Mistral to avoid a crash if a description is not provided to a tool. The Mistral client, in comparison to the OpenAI client, requires the "description" string to be present, even if it's empty.

vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#20139 added a safety-net for releasing prefill blocks that weren't retrieved or notified by a decode worker within a time window (default two minutes).
This PR adds some warning logs both when such expiries occur and when notifications for unrecognized request ids are received.
Also fixes a small l

vllm.title: Use `metavar` to list the choices for a CLI arg when custom values are also accepted
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Before this PR, if a type hint was a Literal[...], then the CLI generation code would place the arguments of the Literal into choices for argparse.
After this PR, if a type hint is Union[str, Literal[...]], then the CLI generation places the argumetns of the Literal into metavar for argparse instead

vllm.title: [AMD][BugFix] Fix omission  of wvSplitK kernel for small batch sizes (1-4) due to torch.compile
vllm.author: rasmith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It turns out that torch.compile was omitting calls to wvSplitK, our skinny gemm, since it was never being called after torch.compile compilation.
Several attempts were made to fix this, such as lifting the conditional logic in rocm_unquantized_gemm into its caller, converting wvSplitK into a custom
